instructions.primary_goal:
Issue: POL-543 — POL-543
Worktree: .
Branch: pol-542-delivery

## Goal
## Objective

Implement `polaris qc resolve` — a CLI command that lets an operator formally resolve a QC repair loop stuck at `operator-review` or `medic-referral`, and wire `finalize`'s QC gate to accept that resolution.

## Context

Parent issue [POL-542](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-542/implement-qc-repair-loop-operator-resolution-path) documents the gap: `finalize`'s QC repair-loop gate (`checkQcRepairLoopGate` in `src/finalize/index.ts:335-368`) only accepts `terminal_outcome` values `pass`, `qc-disabled`, `no-repairable` (`TRUSTED_QC_REPAIR_OUTCOMES`). `operator-review` and `medic-referral` are terminal states an operator must resolve by design (per `src/qc/POLARIS.md`: "operator-review packets settle the terminal operator-review outcome directly without worker dispatch"), but no command exists to record that resolution. The `--bypass-medic` flag on `polaris finalize run` gates an unrelated check (the run-health Medic gate) and has no effect here.

Follow the existing `medic chart create` pattern (`src/cli/medic.ts`) for a CLI command that scaffolds/writes a durable, auditable artifact — this is the closest prior art in the codebase for "an operator records a decision as a file."

## Goal

`polaris qc resolve --cluster-id <id> --outcome <no-repairable|pass> --reason "<text>" [--findings <id1,id2,...>]` writes a resolution artifact, and `finalize`'s gate check recognizes it without requiring `state.qc_repair_loop.terminal_outcome` itself to already be a trusted value.

## Scope

* src/cli/qc.ts (new file — mirror `src/cli/medic.ts` structure)
* src/cli/index.ts (register the new `qc` command)
* src/qc/repair-loop.ts
* src/qc/types.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts (`checkQcRepairLoopGate` and `TRUSTED_QC_REPAIR_OUTCOMES` usage around lines 335-368)
* src/qc/POLARIS.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/SKILL.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris qc resolve --cluster-id <id> --outcome <no-repairable|pass> --reason "<text>"` writes a resolution artifact to `.polaris/clusters/<cluster-id>/qc/repair-rounds/<round>/resolution.json` containing: resolver identity (git user or `$USER`), reason, timestamp, resolved outcome, and the finding IDs from the round's repair-packets.json that this resolution addresses.
- [ ] `--findings <id1,id2,...>` lets the operator explicitly list which finding IDs are covered; if omitted, defaults to all findings referenced by the round's `repair-packets.json`.
- [ ] `checkQcRepairLoopGate` in `src/finalize/index.ts` treats a valid resolution artifact for the current round as equivalent to a trusted `terminal_outcome` — finalize proceeds without mutating `state.qc_repair_loop.terminal_outcome` directly.
- [ ] The resolution artifact requires a non-empty `--reason`; the command errors without one.
- [ ] `.polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md` and `SKILL.md` no longer reference `polaris medic packet <cluster-id>` (which does not exist) for Medic dispatch — replace with the actual mechanism, or remove the reference if none is implemented as part of this change.
- [ ] `src/qc/POLARIS.md` documents the resolution artifact path and the new gate behavior.
- [ ] Tests: gate blocks with no resolution artifact and untrusted `terminal_outcome`; gate passes with a valid resolution artifact; `qc resolve` rejects missing `--reason`; `qc resolve` correctly captures finding IDs from `repair-packets.json` when `--findings` is omitted.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/qc src/finalize src/cli

## Ordering

* None — this is the only child of [POL-542](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-542/implement-qc-repair-loop-operator-resolution-path).

## Non-goals

* Do not change QC finding routing/severity policy (`src/qc/routing.ts`, `src/qc/policy.ts`).
* Do not weaken the gate to silently trust `medic-referral`/`operator-review` without an explicit recorded resolution — a resolution artifact must always be present.
* Do not implement a full Medic-dispatch automation for `medic-referral` — that's a larger feature; this issue only provides the operator resolution path.
* Do not retroactively resolve [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence)'s blocked finalize as part of this change.

## Implementation Discipline
Before writing code, stop at the first rung that holds:

1. Does this need to exist?   → no: skip it (YAGNI)
2. Stdlib does it?            → use it
3. Native platform feature?   → use it
4. Installed dependency?      → use it
5. One line?                  → one line
6. Only then: the minimum that works

Lazy, not negligent: validation, error handling, security, and accessibility are never on the chopping block.

When you defer a simplification for later, mark it inline:
  // ponytail: <what you deferred and why>

## Scope
Touch only:
- src/cli/qc.ts
- src/cli/index.ts
- src/qc/repair-loop.ts
- src/qc/types.ts
- src/finalize/index.ts
- src/qc/POLARIS.md
- .polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md
- .polaris/skills/polaris-run/SKILL.md

## Acceptance Criteria

## Validation
- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/qc src/finalize src/cli

## Commit
[POL-543] POL-543

## Governance
- Do NOT modify open_children or completed_children in /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/current-state.json. The parent runtime (loop continue) owns state transitions.
- Append a telemetry event to /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/polaris-run-pol-542-2026-07-12-001/telemetry.jsonl.

## Worker Progress Telemetry (MANDATORY)
The parent CANNOT see your work. You MUST emit heartbeat events so the parent knows you are alive and making progress.

After EVERY step, append a JSONL event to /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/polaris-run-pol-542-2026-07-12-001/telemetry.jsonl:
```jsonl
{"event":"worker-heartbeat","run_id":"<from packet>","child_id":"POL-543","step_cursor":"<current step>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>","progress_pct":<0-100>,"files_changed":<count>,"current_file":"<file being edited>"}
```

Required heartbeat points:
  1. Immediately after reading this packet (step_cursor: "start")
  2. After verifying state file (step_cursor: "verify")
  3. After EACH file edit (step_cursor: "implement")
  4. After running validation (step_cursor: "validate")
  5. After creating commit (step_cursor: "commit")
  6. Before terminating (step_cursor: "complete" or "failed")

If heartbeats stop, the parent will assume you crashed and may dispatch a replacement worker.

## Approval Escalation (when uncertain)
You have a sealed packet defining your exact scope, allowed files, and validation commands. Use this to decide:

Rule 1 - Auto-approve WITHIN scope (normal work):
  - If the operation touches files within `instructions.allowed_scope` → Just do it
  - If the command is in `instructions.validation_commands` → Just run it
  - Low risk, expected work → No need to ask, emit heartbeat with step_cursor: "implement"

Rule 2 - ESCALATE out-of-scope or high-risk:
  - File outside allowed_scope → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "out-of-scope"
  - Destructive operation (rm -rf, major refactor) → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "destructive"
  - External API calls with cost → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "cost"
  - Changing secrets/.env → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "security"
  - Unclear requirements → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "ambiguous"

To escalate, emit IMMEDIATELY to telemetry:
```jsonl
{"event":"worker-blocked","run_id":"<from packet>","child_id":"POL-543","reason":"needs-approval","approval_type":"<out-of-scope|destructive|cost|security|ambiguous>","description":"<specifically what needs approval>","blocker_id":"<unique-id>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>"}
```

Then: Continue heartbeats every 30s with step_cursor: "blocked-awaiting-approval"
Wait for parent response. If no response in 5 minutes → TERMINATE with approval-timeout.

Remember: Your packet scope is your authority. Within scope = proceed. Outside scope = escalate.

## Route Cognition Delta
After implementation, apply route-local cognition delta — only if something materially changed.

**POLARIS.md** — update only when ALL of the following are true:
  - You touched a file in that folder (not a distant folder).
  - The change materially affects: folder responsibilities, commands/workflows,
    execution constraints, ownership/routing, or operational behavior.
  - The current POLARIS.md content is actually wrong or incomplete as a result.
  DO NOT update POLARIS.md for: formatting fixes, comment changes, tiny refactors,
  internal implementation details, or any change that leaves the operational guidance
  still accurate. When in doubt, do not update.

**SUMMARY.md** — update only when: linked docs/specs changed, canon relationships
  changed, architecture meaning changed, or doctrine/spec linkage changed.
  SUMMARY.md is informational only. Do not create it if missing — that is a hint for
  operators, not a task for workers. Keep it short. Never add operational doctrine.

**Never** scan unrelated folders. **Never** regenerate all route docs.
If no material change occurred, skip this section entirely.

## Report Back
- files changed
- validation results
- commit hash
- blockers

TERMINATE SESSION IMMEDIATELY. Do not select or execute the next child.

issue_context.title: POL-543
issue_context.body:
## Objective

Implement `polaris qc resolve` — a CLI command that lets an operator formally resolve a QC repair loop stuck at `operator-review` or `medic-referral`, and wire `finalize`'s QC gate to accept that resolution.

## Context

Parent issue [POL-542](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-542/implement-qc-repair-loop-operator-resolution-path) documents the gap: `finalize`'s QC repair-loop gate (`checkQcRepairLoopGate` in `src/finalize/index.ts:335-368`) only accepts `terminal_outcome` values `pass`, `qc-disabled`, `no-repairable` (`TRUSTED_QC_REPAIR_OUTCOMES`). `operator-review` and `medic-referral` are terminal states an operator must resolve by design (per `src/qc/POLARIS.md`: "operator-review packets settle the terminal operator-review outcome directly without worker dispatch"), but no command exists to record that resolution. The `--bypass-medic` flag on `polaris finalize run` gates an unrelated check (the run-health Medic gate) and has no effect here.

Follow the existing `medic chart create` pattern (`src/cli/medic.ts`) for a CLI command that scaffolds/writes a durable, auditable artifact — this is the closest prior art in the codebase for "an operator records a decision as a file."

## Goal

`polaris qc resolve --cluster-id <id> --outcome <no-repairable|pass> --reason "<text>" [--findings <id1,id2,...>]` writes a resolution artifact, and `finalize`'s gate check recognizes it without requiring `state.qc_repair_loop.terminal_outcome` itself to already be a trusted value.

## Scope

* src/cli/qc.ts (new file — mirror `src/cli/medic.ts` structure)
* src/cli/index.ts (register the new `qc` command)
* src/qc/repair-loop.ts
* src/qc/types.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts (`checkQcRepairLoopGate` and `TRUSTED_QC_REPAIR_OUTCOMES` usage around lines 335-368)
* src/qc/POLARIS.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/SKILL.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris qc resolve --cluster-id <id> --outcome <no-repairable|pass> --reason "<text>"` writes a resolution artifact to `.polaris/clusters/<cluster-id>/qc/repair-rounds/<round>/resolution.json` containing: resolver identity (git user or `$USER`), reason, timestamp, resolved outcome, and the finding IDs from the round's repair-packets.json that this resolution addresses.
- [ ] `--findings <id1,id2,...>` lets the operator explicitly list which finding IDs are covered; if omitted, defaults to all findings referenced by the round's `repair-packets.json`.
- [ ] `checkQcRepairLoopGate` in `src/finalize/index.ts` treats a valid resolution artifact for the current round as equivalent to a trusted `terminal_outcome` — finalize proceeds without mutating `state.qc_repair_loop.terminal_outcome` directly.
- [ ] The resolution artifact requires a non-empty `--reason`; the command errors without one.
- [ ] `.polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md` and `SKILL.md` no longer reference `polaris medic packet <cluster-id>` (which does not exist) for Medic dispatch — replace with the actual mechanism, or remove the reference if none is implemented as part of this change.
- [ ] `src/qc/POLARIS.md` documents the resolution artifact path and the new gate behavior.
- [ ] Tests: gate blocks with no resolution artifact and untrusted `terminal_outcome`; gate passes with a valid resolution artifact; `qc resolve` rejects missing `--reason`; `qc resolve` correctly captures finding IDs from `repair-packets.json` when `--findings` is omitted.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/qc src/finalize src/cli

## Ordering

* None — this is the only child of [POL-542](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-542/implement-qc-repair-loop-operator-resolution-path).

## Non-goals

* Do not change QC finding routing/severity policy (`src/qc/routing.ts`, `src/qc/policy.ts`).
* Do not weaken the gate to silently trust `medic-referral`/`operator-review` without an explicit recorded resolution — a resolution artifact must always be present.
* Do not implement a full Medic-dispatch automation for `medic-referral` — that's a larger feature; this issue only provides the operator resolution path.
* Do not retroactively resolve [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence)'s blocked finalize as part of this change.
